<!-- generated by: reviewer | run: 2026-02-24T11:25 | evidence: agents.yaml available lists vs skills/{agent}/ directory contents -->
# Seed Skill Gaps Across All Agents

## What I'm Seeing

The factory blueprint (`universe/synthesis/factory-blueprint.md`, "Seed Skills in Detail" section) describes a minimum seed set of skills that should exist before agents run. `agents.yaml` references these skills in each agent's `available` list. But 14 of them have no `SKILL.md` content — their directories either contain only `.gitkeep` or don't exist at all.

Specifically:

| Agent | Listed in `available` | Exists | Missing |
|-------|----------------------|--------|---------|
| researcher | research-approach, source-evaluation, recommendation-format | research-approach | source-evaluation, recommendation-format |
| spec | nlspec-format, domain-interview, spec-patterns | nlspec-format | domain-interview, spec-patterns |
| builder | implementation-approach, convergence, tool-use | implementation-approach | convergence, tool-use |
| verifier | verification-protocol, satisfaction-scoring, scenario-evaluation, failure-reporting | verification-protocol | satisfaction-scoring, scenario-evaluation, failure-reporting |
| librarian | knowledge-curation, pattern-detection, memory-hygiene | knowledge-curation | pattern-detection, memory-hygiene |
| operator | git-workflow, ci-cd, dependency-management, monitoring | git-workflow | ci-cd, dependency-management, monitoring |

Each agent currently operates with only its first seed skill. The blueprint describes the others as part of the "minimum seed set" — the skills "written by a human because the agents don't exist yet to write them."

## What I Want to See

All 14 missing seed skills created with content faithful to the descriptions in the factory blueprint. Each skill file at `skills/{agent}/{skill-name}/SKILL.md` should encode the behavioral guidance described in the blueprint section for that agent. The skills don't need to be long — the blueprint's own examples are 15-40 lines each — but they need to exist so that agents loading their `available` skills actually get guidance.
